title: parsing of `--volume` option `idmap` succeeds but parsing of option `idmap=true` fails with `Error: unknown option "true"`
body:
### Issue Description

On
https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-run.1.html
under the section
_Options specific to bind and glob:_
there is the text

_idmap: true or false (default if unspecified: false). If true, create an idmapped mount to the target user namespace in the container._

Here is the relevant markdown source:

* https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/2aacd4e212525db4ee06be8e44e4405400d4df9d/docs/source/markdown/options/mount.md?plain=1#L56

The behaviour of __podman run__ does not match the documentation. Instead __podman run__ shows this behaviour

| option | parsing result |
| --        |  ---   |
| `idmap` | success |
| `idmap=true` | failure |


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue

1. `sudo -i`
2. Create the file _/root/test.bash_ containing
   ```
   #!/bin/bash
   set -o errexit
   set -o nounset
   dir=$(mktemp -d)
   chmod 777 "$dir"
   option=$1
   podman run --rm \
            --userns auto \
            --user 1000:1000 \
            --mount "type=bind,source=${dir},destination=/vol,Z=true,${option}" \
              docker.io/library/alpine touch /vol/file
   ls -ln "$dir/file"
   ```
3. Run command
   ```
   bash test.bash idmap=true
   ```
   The following output is printed
   ```
   Error: unknown option "true"
   ```
4. Run command
   ```
   bash test.bash idmap
   ```
   The following output is printed
   ```
   -rw-r--r--. 1 1000 1000 0 Oct 12 21:09 /tmp/tmp.dRdruZ0jI3/file
   ```



### Describe the results you received

Step 3 fails.

### Describe the results you expected

I would have expected Step 3 to succeed and print something similar to what is printed in Step 4.

### podman info output


### Privileged Or Rootless

### Additional information

An example of this bug:

https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/22535#discussion-6581853

comments.body:
it is a documentation issue, we need to fix the documentation to describe the podman behavior.

Would you mind opening a PR to fix it with what you've already found?
Ok, I'll give it a try.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Maybe revamping the structure in _mount.md_ would make it easier to document it?

Something like adding a table
```
| option            | bind | devpts | globs | image | ramfs | tmpfs | volume |
| ----------------- | ---- | ------ | ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- | -------|
| bind-nonrecursive |   x  |        |   x   |       |       |       |        |
| bind-propagation  |   x  |        |   x   |       |       |       |        |
| chown (U)         |   x  |        |   x   |       |       |       |        |
| destination (dst) |   x  |   x    |   x   |   x   |  x    |   x   |   x    |
| gid               |      |   x    |       |       |       |       |        |
| idmap             |   x  |        |   x   |       |       |       |   x    |
| max               |      |   x    |       |       |       |       |        |
| mode              |      |   x    |       |       |       |       |        |
| no-dereference    |   x  |        |   x   |       |       |       |        |
| notmpcopyup       |      |        |       |       |  x    |   x   |        |
| readonly (ro)     |   x  |   x    |   x   |   x   |  x    |   x   |   x    |
| readwrite (rw)    |   x  |   x    |   x   |   x   |  x    |   x   |   x    |
| relabel           |   x  |        |   x   |       |       |       |        |
| source (src)      |   x  |   x    |   x   |   x   |  x    |   x   |   x    |
| subpath           |      |        |       |   x   |       |       |        |
| tmpcopyup         |      |        |       |       |  x    |   x   |        |
| tmpfs-mode        |      |        |       |       |  x    |   x   |        |
| tmpfs-size        |      |        |       |       |  x    |   x   |        |
| uid               |      |   x    |       |       |       |       |        |
```

And then have a flat list describing all the options (without the grouping into different subsets).

I could try out to see how it looks like.

Some months ago I started drafting a JSON file that could contain all information about the volume options.
It's very sketchy so don't look into the details:
https://github.com/eriksjolund/podman/tree/fix-issue-24249/docs/source/markdown/volume-options
Generator code has not yet been written.
The structure of the JSON file is also in drafting stage.

Still sketchy design idea:
input:
https://github.com/eriksjolund/podman/blob/fix-issue-24249/docs/source/markdown/volume-options/mount-options.json

generated output:
https://github.com/eriksjolund/podman/blob/fix-issue-24249/docs/source/markdown/volume-options/output.md

sketchy python script
https://github.com/eriksjolund/podman/blob/fix-issue-24249/docs/source/markdown/volume-options/generate-docs.py
Keeping this open because #29129 fixes the immediate incorrect documentation issue; we still need to address the coverage table idea discussed here.
